name: Family Sports Center Ice Arena

Two rinks at Keystone: Lake Rink (Lakeside Village) and Dercum Square Ice Rink (River Run Village - smaller, free admission). Note: admission to Lake Rink includes all-day access.

NOTE: The Lake Loops trails on the reservoir did not open in 2025-26 season due to late freeze. Check townofdillon.com for current season status.

notes: Two NHL sheets. Multi-purpose arena serving south Omaha. Public skating sessions and hockey leagues available alongside events.

name: Sidner Ice Arena
price_public_skate: ⚠️ Verify pricing before visiting — check the rink website or call ahead
notes: Recently renovated concrete-base NHL rink. Modern concessions, upgraded player benches and comfortable lounge. Transforms into event space April–June.

name: Omaha Riverfront Skate Ribbon (Outdoor)
price_public_skate: ⚠️ Verify pricing before visiting — check the rink website or call ahead
notes: Trail-style outdoor ribbon rink along the Omaha riverfront. Free to skate. Steps from the Gene Leahy Mall and Kiewit Luminarium. Popular weekend destination.

name: Winter Park Village Pond
parking: Free small gondola lot; main resort parking garage also available
price_public_skate: FREE with own skates | Skate rental ~$19/hr at Adventure & Supply Co.
notes: Picturesque outdoor pond rink in the heart of Winter Park Resort's alpine village. FREE admission with your own skates. Skate rentals available at Adventure & Supply Co. (~$19/hr). Glow Night Cosmic Skating every Thu/Fri/Sat 5-7pm with DJ and colorful lights. Open mid-November to mid-April. Views of the Continental Divide. Ice maintained daily by resort. | On-site shop: Adventure & Supply Co.

name: Edwards Freedom Park Outdoor Ice Rinks
price_public_skate: FREE — no admission charge
price_stick_puck: FREE — drop-in on the hockey rink
notes: Community outdoor ice rinks at Edwards Freedom Park, managed by Mountain Recreation District with dedicated volunteer Tom Boyd. Now in their 4th+ season. Two rinks: a larger hockey rink and a smaller general skating rink, both built on turf using a specialized board/liner system. FREE to skate. Loaner skates available in storage boxes near baseball dugouts (first-come, limited supply). Golf-cart Zamboni maintained by volunteers. Part of a beloved neighborhood tradition — kids skate after school, adults at lunch. Contact Mountain Rec at (970) 766-5555.

name: Montrose Rotary Park Ice Rink
address: Rotary Park, 1020 S Townsend Ave, Montrose, CO 81401
price_public_skate: FREE
notes: Natural non-refrigerated ice pond at Rotary Park in Montrose. City of Montrose Parks & Cemeteries maintains the rink when conditions allow — typically 1–6 weeks per winter depending on weather. 100x200 ft cleared area. FREE admission, NO skate rentals on-site. Ice safety verified by city staff (3-inch drill test). Locals drop in for hockey and broomball. Skate at your own risk per posted signage. Contact City of Montrose Parks at (970) 249-7505.

name: Pagosa Multi-Purpose Pavilion Ice Rink
price_public_skate: Check skatepagosa.org for current season rates | Season passes & punch cards available
notes: Pagosa Springs' community rink at South Town Park Pavilion. Outdoor seasonal rink with a full program: public skating, drop-in hockey (Saturdays), broomball (Sundays), curling with Riff Raff Brewing (Tuesdays), and figure skating. Skate rentals available. Season passes and punch cards available. Private lessons available — call (970) 398-2337. Email: skatepagosa@icloud.com. Pair with a soak at The Springs Resort natural hot spring complex nearby — a perfect post-skate tradition. Also note: free natural ice skating on Town ponds behind River Center when thick enough (4+ inches), no fee, no rentals.

name: Ben Boeke Ice Arena
notes: Municipality of Anchorage community hockey facility. Two sheets. Managed by O'Malley Sports. Full hockey programming and public skating. Shares parcel address with Sullivan Arena — east side entrance. GPS: 61.20763, -149.87944.

name: Big Dipper Ice Arena
notes: Fairbanks North Star Borough Parks & Recreation facility. Community ice rink. ⚠️ Website unverified.

name: Big Lake Recreation Center

name: Dempsey Anderson Ice Arena
notes: Municipality of Anchorage facility. Two sheets. Managed by O'Malley Sports. Public skating, stick & puck, hockey leagues.

name: George M. Sullivan Arena

name: Harry J. McDonald Memorial Center

Also referred to as Kelley Connect Ice Center in some directories. Home of Alaska Oilers, Alaska Wolves, Team Alaska youth hockey. Hockey House training center on second level.

name: Treadwell Arena

Public skating, stick & puck, pond hockey, figure skating, speed skating clinics, youth and adult hockey. Public skate: $7 adult / $6 under 18 / $5 senior. Skate rental included in admission.

name: UAA Wells Fargo Sports Complex Ice Rink

name: Pelham Civic Complex & Ice Arena
notes: Two NHL-sized sheets. Year-round public skating, youth and adult hockey leagues, figure skating programs. Home of Birmingham Bulls (SPHL) practice facility. Also hosts large-scale events, corporate functions, and community celebrations. Located off I-65 in Pelham.

name: Arkansas Skatium

name: AZ Ice Arcadia

notes: USHL arena. Home of Des Moines Buccaneers. Hockey-primary; limited public skating access.

name: Casey's Center
notes: Renamed from Wells Fargo Arena to Casey's Center in June 2025. 15,181-seat AHL arena. Home of Iowa Wild (AHL affiliate of Minnesota Wild). Also home to Iowa Wolves (NBA G League) and Iowa Barnstormers. Opened 2005. Part of Iowa Events Center complex. No public skating — hockey events only.

name: Cedar Rapids Ice Arena
notes: Opened Jan 8, 2000. City-owned. 3,850-seat NHL sheet (home of USHL Cedar Rapids RoughRiders) + Olympic-size public/figure skating sheet. Two sheets on same campus — CSV incorrectly listed as two separate locations. Home of Cedar Rapids Hockey Association, Cedar Rapids High School RoughRiders, Eastern Iowa Figure Skating Club, Cedar Rapids Curling Club, Iowa Speed Skating Association. U of Iowa Hawkeyes club hockey plays here. Formerly ImOn Ice Arena — naming rights expired 2024. New sponsor name not yet announced as of publication.

name: Coral Ridge Ice Arena
notes: Year-round community rink serving Iowa City and Coralville. Public skating, hockey lessons, youth hockey.

name: Five Flags Civic Center Ice
notes: Multi-purpose civic center. Ice installed seasonally for events only — not a dedicated year-round rink. Limited hockey programming.

name: IBP Ice Center
notes: Founded 1998. 38,000 sq ft facility. NHL sheet (85'x200'), 900 padded seats. Year-round: youth hockey, public skating, tournaments, figure skating, off-season inline skating and indoor soccer. City of Sioux City operated.

name: Mason City Arena
notes: NAHL venue. Home of Mason City Toros. Hockey-primary with limited public skating.

name: Metro Ice Sports Facility
notes: Two-sheet facility serving Des Moines metro. Full hockey programming: public skate, youth hockey, adult leagues.

name: Mid-America Center
notes: Multi-purpose arena. Hockey available via rentals only — not a dedicated year-round ice facility. Part of Iowa Events Center network.

name: Mystique Community Ice Center
notes: USHL arena. Home of Dubuque Fighting Saints. Full community programming including public skating, youth hockey, and figure skating.

name: The River's Edge Ice Arena
notes: Quad Cities' primary ice facility on the Iowa side (Davenport). Full hockey programming: public skate, youth hockey, adult leagues. ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER URL — links to city site; verify dedicated arena website.

name: Tyson Events Center
notes: USHL arena. Home of Sioux City Musketeers. Hockey-primary; limited public skating access. Large event venue.

name: Xtream Arena
notes: ECHL arena opened 2021. Home of Iowa Heartlanders. Hockey-primary; limited public skating access.

name: Young Arena
notes: USHL arena. Home of Waterloo Black Hawks. Full hockey programming including public skate, adult open hockey, and youth hockey. Year-round.

name: Frontier Ice Arena
notes: 501(c)(3) nonprofit owned by KYRO (Kootenai Youth Recreation Organization). NHL-size rink. 5 locker rooms. Serves Eastern WA, North ID, Western MT. Hockey, curling, figure skating, sled hockey, broomball. 25 min from Spokane. ⚠️ CSV had address as 3525 W Seltice — corrected to 3519 per Arena Guide.

name: Idaho Central Arena
notes: ECHL arena opened 1997. Home of Idaho Steelheads (ECHL) since 1997. 5,002-seat capacity for hockey. Downtown Boise. Also known as CenturyLink Arena, Qwest Arena, Bank of America Centre historically. ⚠️ CSV incorrectly classified as event-based with no permanent ice — corrected: permanent ECHL home rink.

name: Idaho IceWorld
notes: City of Boise municipal facility. Two NHL-regulation rinks. LiveBarn venue. Sandwich and pizza restaurants, arcade, pro shop. Adult and youth hockey leagues, figure skating, learn-to-skate and learn-to-play hockey, public skating daily.

name: Joe Marmo/Wayne Lehto Ice Arena
notes: Located in Tautphaus Park, southeastern Idaho's premier ice facility. Regulation ice surface, rental shop, snack bar, locker rooms. ⚠️ Not in source CSV — added from Visit Idaho rink directory. ⚠️ Website unverified — contact city recreation at (208) 612-8480.

name: LC Ice Arena
notes: Home of Lewis Clark Amateur Hockey Association (LCAHA), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Official practice facility of WSU Hockey. Home of the LC Lightning. ⚠️ Not in source CSV — added from Arena Guide. ⚠️ Phone unverified.

name: Manchester Ice & Event Centre
notes: NHL-size regulation rink. Grandstand seating. TJ's Cutting Edge Café on-site. Located across from Payette Lake in downtown McCall. Daily public skate, skate rentals. Full hockey programming.

name: Palouse Ice Rink
notes: Three-quarter size ice sheet (smaller than NHL regulation). Seasonal: ice Oct through early Apr, roller skating late Apr through mid-Sep. Hockey, curling, figure skating, broomball, Science on Ice. Located at Latah County Fairgrounds. Only ice rink on the Palouse. ⚠️ Not in source CSV — added from Arena Guide and Visit Idaho.

name: Addison Ice Arena
notes: High-volume dual-sheet arena with strong Tier II and AAA programming. Home to Elite Edge Skating Academy.

name: Arctic Ice Arena
notes: Three sheets. Year-round programming. Bar and grill, pro shop, skate sharpening. Home to Chicago Fury and Orland Park Vikings youth hockey (Arctic Jr. Fury program). One of the largest and busiest rinks in the southwest suburbs. 440,000+ annual visitors. Public skate, birthday parties, figure skating, adult leagues.

name: Barrington Ice Arena
notes: Home of Barrington Redwings. Strong youth hockey programs.

name: Bloomington Ice Center
notes: Operated by Bloomington Parks & Recreation. Full-size NHL sheet (200x85). Adjacent to Grossinger Motors Arena (6,000-seat ECHL venue, home of Bloomington Bison). Snack bar, skate rental, lockers, skate sharpening. Programs: Learn to Skate, Learn to Play Hockey, youth and adult hockey leagues, freestyle, stick & puck, pick-up hockey, open skate, curling leagues. Home to ISU Redbirds Hockey Club (ACHA). Located inside Grossinger Motors Arena complex; entrance and parking on Roosevelt Ave.

name: Canlan Sports Romeoville
notes: Formerly International Ice Centre. Operated by Canlan Sports. Home to youth and adult hockey leagues, figure skating, and public skating.

name: Canlan Sports West Dundee
notes: Formerly Leafs Ice Centre. Three NHL sheets. Managed by Canlan Sports (formerly Fairview Facilities Management). Jerry's Pro Shop, BlueLine Bar and Grill restaurant. Off-ice training. Home to Jr. Leafs Youth Hockey (formerly Dundee Dynamites), Leafs Hockey Club, Silver Leafs Figure Skating, Chicago Fusion Synchronized Skating, Chicago Cougars (USPHL), NIU Huskies club hockey, and several HS programs (Crystal Lake South, Mid Valley, Kings). Working relationship with Chicago Blackhawks and Chicago Wolves.

name: Carlson Ice Arena
notes: Two-sheet facility (Carlson Ice Arena + Carlson Arctic Ice Arena) on the same campus. Rockford Park District. Public skate, hockey, figure skating. Strong youth, high school, and adult leagues including Rockford Hockey Club.

name: Centennial Ice Rinks
notes: Opened 1977. Operated by Wilmette Park District inside the Centennial Recreation Complex (shared with Wilmette Tennis Club and Family Aquatic Center). One full NHL sheet + one studio sheet. ISI National award-winning facility for management and ice-skating programs. Named #1 ice rink by North Shore Magazine readers. Instructs 3,000+ skaters/year. Home to Wilmette Jr. Trevians Hockey (WHA), Wilmette Tribe Hockey Club, and New Trier High School Hockey Club.

name: Center Ice of DuPage
notes: Community rink serving Glen Ellyn and surrounding DuPage County communities.

name: Crystal Ice House
notes: Two-sheet arena serving McHenry County. Strong youth and high school hockey presence. Home to Crystal Lake Yellowjackets youth hockey.

name: Darien Sportsplex
notes: Operated by Darien Park District. Three NHL sheets of ice plus full-size indoor fieldhouse. Hockey pro shop, hockey training center, concession stand, meeting room, party rooms, mini arcade. Home to multiple youth and adult leagues. Strong Tier II programming.

name: David S. Palmer Arena
notes: Multi-purpose arena with indoor ice sheet. Hockey, figure skating, and public sessions. Also hosts concerts and events.

name: Decatur Civic Center Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal ice (fall–spring). Ice removed during off-season and reinstalled annually. Community rink serving Decatur and Macon County. Active programming including public skating.

name: Downers Grove Ice Arena
notes: Long-standing community rink with strong youth and high school hockey participation.

name: East Alton Ice Arena
notes: Community indoor rink serving the River Bend / Metro East area. Youth hockey, figure skating, and public skate. Long-standing facility serving East Alton, Wood River, and Alton.

name: Fifth Third Arena
spectator_notes: Championship Rink seats 2,000; practice rinks seat ~275 each.
notes: Opened 2017. Official practice home of the Chicago Blackhawks (NHL). $65M expansion opened early 2026, adding 2 new sheets for a total of 4. Expansion includes Championship Rink (2,000 seats) — home of Chicago Steel (USHL) starting 2026-27 season — plus Rocky's Bar (tribute to Rocky Wirtz), Blackhawks Hall of Fame, lounge spaces, skyline patio, and all-day café. 440,000+ annual visitors. Each rink seats ~275 with ADA-friendly boards and synthetic ice for sled hockey. Pro shop, off-ice training, public skating, adult leagues, youth programming.

name: Fox Valley Ice Arena
notes: Two sheets: 1 NHL (200x85) + 1 Olympic (200x100). Official Chicago Blackhawks rink partner. Former home of Chicago Steel (USHL) — team moving to Fifth Third Arena Championship Rink for 2026-27. Also former home of Chicago Freeze, Bluesmen, and Hitmen. On-site: full-service pro shop, Fox Valley Fitness Center (second floor), Chelios' Pub and Grill (17 TVs). Programs: AA Cyclones, youth hockey, adult leagues, figure skating, public skating, drop-in hockey.

name: Franklin Park Ice Arena
notes: Operated by Park District of Franklin Park. Public skating ($15 adult, $10 children under 12 during Sunday Family Skate). Year-round hockey and skating programs.

name: Glacier Ice Arena
notes: Dual-sheet arena serving Vernon Hills, Libertyville, and surrounding communities.

name: Glenview Community Ice Center
spectator_notes: Main rink seats 550; second rink seats 150.
notes: Year-round. Three sheets: 2 NHL (200x85) + 1 studio. Main rink: 550 seats, 6 locker rooms. Second rink: 150 seats, 5 locker rooms. On-site: Offensive Zone Hockey Pro Shop, North Branch restaurant (seats 50), Joe Donut grab-and-go, dryland training area, dance/birthday studio. Home to Glenview Stars youth hockey and area high school teams. One of the premier multi-sheet facilities in Illinois.

name: Glenview Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Consistently maintained seasonal outdoor rink with boards. Used for community skating and pickup.

name: Granite City Ice Rink
notes: ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER URL — generic city homepage. ⚠️ Operating season and current status unconfirmed; sparse online presence. Call before publishing. Community indoor rink at Wilson Park (2900 Benton St). Also known as Wilson Park Ice Rink.

name: H-F Ice Arena
notes: Central South Suburban rink with strong high school (Homewood-Flossmoor Vikings) and youth hockey participation.

name: Heartland Ice Arena
notes: Community rink serving Lincolnwood and surrounding North Side communities. Also listed as American Heartland Ice Arena.

name: Highland Park Centennial Arena
notes: Community arena serving Highland Park and surrounding North Shore communities.

name: Ice Valley Centre Ice Arena
notes: Operated by Kankakee Valley Park District. NHL sheet. Breakaway Café concessions, Game Zone arcade, 4 private party/meeting rooms, state-of-the-art fitness center. Programs: public skate, learn to skate, figure skating, intro to hockey, youth hockey (Kankakee Coyotes), adult hockey, curling (Ice Valley Curling), drop-in, freestyle. Little Blackhawks Learn to Play partner. 50 miles south of Chicago.

name: Inwood Ice Arena
notes: Central rink for the Joliet area. Home to youth, high school, and adult leagues including the Joliet Jaguars. Also known as Joliet Junior College Ice Arena.

name: Johnny's IceHouse East
notes: Iconic adult hockey hub near the United Center. One slightly-smaller-than-regulation sheet. Stanley Club Bar & Grill overlooks the ice. Pro shop (skate sharpening, tape, supplies). Figure Skate Chicago teaches lessons here. Paid parking available nearby (spot hero). Home to one of the largest adult hockey leagues in the Midwest across multiple skill divisions.

name: Johnny's IceHouse West
notes: Opened Feb 2010. Two sheets: main NHL-size rink + Annex Studio Rink (added 2014, with mezzanine viewing and dryland training). Former official Blackhawks practice facility (2010–2018). Heated indoor parking garage. 4 large locker rooms with individual stalls and showers, plus dedicated women's locker room. Stanley Club Bar & Grill overlooks ice. Pro shop. One of the most iconic adult hockey facilities in the Midwest.

name: Jr. Bulldogs Ice Arena
notes: Park Ridge Park District rink. Home of Bulldogs Hockey.

name: Lake Forest Academy Ice Rink
notes: ⚠️ Locker room count inferred from facility layout; not published. Prep school rink. Used by local clubs. Limited public access.

name: Lake Forest College Ice Rink
notes: NCAA DIII hockey (Lake Forest Foresters). Limited public access — call ahead. Campus Wi-Fi available.

name: McFetridge Sports Center
notes: Chicago Park District facility. Two sheets: main rink and studio rink. Adult recreational hockey leagues on main rink; Stick & Puck on studio rink (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sun). Figure skating classes, synchronized skating, open skate. Skate rental available ($3). Skate sharpening in pro shop. Open year-round except August.

name: McKendree Metro Rec Plex
notes: Opened January 2017. State-of-the-art full-service complex. Poettker Arena houses 2 NHL sheets with spectator seating; one rink convertible for concerts/events. Also includes 10-lane championship swimming pool, fitness center, walking track, group fitness studios, child care. Home to McKendree University hockey (men's D2, D3, and women's D1 ACHA) and Southern Illinois Ice Hawks youth club. Membership-based with day passes available. Only indoor ice facility in O'Fallon, IL.

name: Morgan Park Sports Center
notes: South Side's only year-round indoor rink. Chicago Park District facility serving Beverly, Morgan Park, and surrounding South Side neighborhoods. Heavy youth hockey + figure skating.

name: Nelson Center
notes: Two-sheet facility serving Springfield and surrounding central Illinois communities.

name: Nelson Sports Complex
notes: Operated by Rolling Meadows Park District. One sheet. Companion facility to West Meadows Ice Arena (same city). Hockey leagues, skating programs, and public skate.

name: Nicholas Sportsplex / Mt. Prospect Ice Arena
notes: 104,000 sq ft. Three NHL sheets (North, South, and Reapers rink — third sheet added for Chicago Reapers Tier I club). Home to Northwest Chargers (Tier II), Chicago Reapers (Tier I), and Northern Express youth hockey, plus Saint Viator HS and Notre Dame College Prep. On-site: Jerry's Hockey pro shop, Big Fish's Icehouse / Bricks Wood Fired Pizza Café (second floor restaurant/bar), Health Performance Institute (Illinois Bone & Joint Institute). Parking for 600+ guests.

name: Niles IceLand
notes: Long-standing community rink serving Niles, Morton Grove, and Park Ridge.

name: North Shore Ice Arena
notes: Two sheets: one full NHL sheet + one studio sheet. High-volume facility used heavily for Tier II and AAA programming. Home to Winnetka Hockey Club and North Shore Hockey Club travel programs.

name: Northbrook Sports Center
notes: Operated by Northbrook Park District for 40+ years. Two year-round NHL sheets. Dance rooms, pro shop, concession stand, party/meeting rentals, outdoor swimming pool. Has produced national-level figure skaters. Home to Northbrook Bluehawks youth hockey, speedskating club, ISI and USFS competitive teams, synchronized skating teams, and SPICE program (skaters with special needs). One of the busiest sports venues on Chicago's North Shore.

name: Oak Forest Ice Arena
notes: Community rink with strong youth and high school programs. Anchors the far-south corridor.

name: Oak Lawn Ice Arena
notes: Operated by Oak Lawn Park District. Year-round. Single sheet with large viewing platform (capacity ~1,100). Recently underwent major makeover (2026). Party rooms, locker rooms, vending machines, private ice rentals. Serves hockey teams, figure skaters, and recreational skaters.

name: Oakton Ice Arena
notes: Central community rink for Park Ridge and Des Plaines youth hockey.

name: Owens Center
notes: Two-sheet arena serving Peoria, East Peoria, and surrounding communities.

name: Pavilion Ice Arena
notes: Elk Grove Park District facility. Longstanding community rink; figure skating and hockey.

name: R.P. Lumber Center
notes: Opened June 2022. City of Edwardsville facility. NHL regulation sheet (200x85). Also features 175-meter indoor track, fitness room, teen center, and bookable community rooms. LiveBarn equipped. Adjacent to Edwardsville High School and Metro-East Lutheran HS. Year-round hockey, figure skating, public skate, lessons.

name: Robert Crown Community Center & Ice Complex
spectator_notes: 130,000 sq ft building; ADA compliant throughout.
notes: Completed 2020. Award-winning 130,000 sq ft community center ($55M project). Two full NHL sheets. Also includes library branch, two full gyms, 200-meter running track, dance studio, 9 locker rooms, art room, childcare, outdoor sports fields. Home to Northwestern Wildcats men's hockey and synchronized skating teams (practice). Evanston Township HS hockey. Public skating $3–$7. Named best project by American Public Works Association (2021) and Chicago AIA top design.

name: Rocket Ice Arena
notes: Community rink serving Bolingbrook and surrounding southwest suburban communities.

name: Rosemont Ice Arena
notes: Newly opened state-of-the-art facility. Two regulation NHL sheets, parking for ~400. Upscale restaurant and bar with floor-to-ceiling glass windows overlooking both rinks, social lounge, private dining and party rooms, elevated grab-and-go market. Home to Tier 1 Mission Girls Hockey and multiple clubs. Public skate Fridays and Sundays.

name: SIU Ice Arena
notes: Seasonal facility. Used primarily for SIU club hockey and community skating.

name: Schaumburg Ice Arena
notes: Schaumburg Park District facility. Strong youth hockey and figure skating programs.

name: Seven Bridges Ice Arena
notes: Three ice surfaces with public skate 7 days a week. Home to Seven Bridges Skating Academy and Sabres Hockey Association. One of the most active rinks in the western suburbs.

name: Skatium Ice Arena
notes: Community rink operated by Skokie Park District. Home to Skokie Flyers youth hockey. Strong youth and adult programming. One full sheet + one studio sheet. One of the region's busiest figure skating centers.

name: Southwest Ice Arena
notes: Family-owned. Three rinks. Lobby with fireplace and big-screen TV. Skating lessons for children year-round. Strong community atmosphere serving Crestwood, Alsip, and surrounding communities.

name: The Edge Ice Arena
notes: Three sheets: Edge II (735 E Jefferson), Edge on John (545 John St), and Water's Edge. Former official practice home of Chicago Blackhawks (NHL). Former home of Chicago Steel (USHL, 2000-2015). 3,000-seat main arena. Home to Chicago Blues adult hockey leagues, Roosevelt Lakers college hockey (ACHA D1 men's & women's), and multiple HS programs. Also houses The Water's Edge Aquatic Center.

name: Top Shelf Ice Arena
notes: Newer facility serving northern Lake County. Strong youth and adult programming.

name: Triphahn Center Ice Arena
notes: Operated by Hoffman Estates Park District (Scott R. Triphahn Community Center). Two NHL sheets. Official practice facility of the Chicago Wolves (AHL). One of the premier Chicagoland facilities. Figure skating and hockey programs ranked among the finest in the area. Public skate, freestyle, skate rental available.

name: Twin Rinks Ice Pavilion
notes: Built 1997. Two NHL sheets. Adult hockey in six levels of play, beginner skating school, youth hockey classes and leagues. No public skating offered.

name: University of Illinois Ice Arena
notes: University of Illinois Campus Recreation facility. Home to UIUC club hockey, figure skating, and public skating programs. Full event calendar year-round. Facility under long-term renovation discussion; remains open and active.

name: West Meadows Ice Arena
notes: Operated by Rolling Meadows Park District. Two NHL-size sheets. Public skate ($10 adult / $8 children), $4 skate rental. Companion facility to Nelson Sports Complex (same city).

name: Winnetka Ice Arena
notes: Built 1972. Operated by Winnetka Park District. Seasonal (September–May). One full NHL sheet with bleacher seating, skate rental, lounge. LiveBarn equipped. Home to Winnetka Warriors (WHC), North Shore HC, and North Shore Warhawks youth hockey. Strong figure skating programs.

name: Winnetka Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink adjacent to the Winnetka Ice Arena. Maintained annually by Winnetka Park District with posted hours.

name: Arctic Zone Iceplex
notes: Single-sheet indoor rink (185'x85'). 4 locker rooms, pro shop, snack bar, party rooms, fitness gym. Year-round. Owned by same group as Carmel Ice Skadium. Home of Indianapolis Youth Hockey Association and Ice Skating Club of Indianapolis.

name: Bulldog Park Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink at Bulldog Park in downtown Crown Point. City-operated (Crown Point Parks). Seasonal Nov–Feb. No hockey programming.

name: Carmel Ice Skadium
notes: Founded 1974. Two NHL-size sheets, 1,350 seating. Home of Indianapolis Youth Hockey Association and Ice Skating Club of Indianapolis. Pro shop, snack bar, party rooms. Public skating daily year-round.

name: Compton Family Ice Arena
registration_label: Notre Dame Hockey / Irish Youth Hockey
notes: Premier college facility opened 2011. 5,022-seat main arena with NHL sheet + Olympic sheet. Home of Notre Dame Fighting Irish hockey. Public skating, curling, intramurals. On-site: Irish Hockey Shop, O'Brien's Irish Pub. Chicago Blackhawks practice here. 700 rental skates.

name: Corteva Coliseum Ice Rink
notes: Formerly Indiana Farmers Coliseum. Public skating, youth hockey, figure skating available. Admission $10, skate rental $3, spectators free. Located at Indiana State Fairgrounds. Home of Indy Fuel (ECHL).

name: Frankfort Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink. Frankfort Parks operated. No dedicated phone.

name: Hamilton Community Center & Ice Arena
notes: Built 1958, designed by architect Harry Weese. Enclosed 1975-76. Renovated 2015 ($2.8M). NHL sheet + studio practice rink (2 sheets total). Year-round. Home of Columbus Icemen HS hockey, Columbus Flames youth hockey, Lincoln Center Skate Club, Full Throttle Speedskating, Columbus Broomball Association. Home of Indiana Sentinels (FPHL) starting 2025-26. Columbus Parks & Recreation operated.

name: Ice Box Skating Rink
notes: Three-sheet facility. 501(c)(3) nonprofit. LiveBarn on all three rinks. Full pro shop, 5,500 sq ft dryland training facility, private party room. Home of Irish Youth Hockey. Serves Michiana region (South Bend / Mishawaka). Community access and grassroots hockey programs.

name: Indy Fuel Tank
notes: Two NHL-size indoor sheets. Home of Indy Fuel youth hockey, high school, and adult leagues. Public skating daily. Learn to Skate program. Best of Fishers Award 2024.

name: Jeffersonville Riverfront Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink along the Ohio River riverfront. Jeffersonville Parks operated. Seasonal pop-up; no dedicated phone.

name: Madison Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink at Bicentennial Park. Madison Parks operated. No dedicated phone.

name: Merrifield Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink with boards at Merrifield Park. Mishawaka Parks operated. Borderline hockey-capable but not regulation size.

name: Michigan City Ice Rink
notes: Outdoor lighted facility at Washington Park. 70'x100' seasonal sheet with restrooms, benches, and warming barrel. Open weather permitting. Ice conditions hotline: (873) 1406 ext 369.

name: Midwest Training & Ice Center
notes: NW Indiana's largest training facility. Olympic-size indoor sheet. 1,500 spectator capacity. Home to Midwest Blackbirds youth hockey and Illiana High School Hockey League. On-site: Center Ice Hockey Supply pro shop, Championz Cafe, 6,000 sq ft fitness center with saunas.

name: NIBCO Water & Ice Park
notes: Seasonal outdoor ribbon-style rink at NIBCO Water & Ice Park. Elkhart Parks operated. No dedicated phone — contact Elkhart Parks.

name: New Albany Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink. New Albany Parks operated. No dedicated phone — contact Parks Dept.

name: PSM Icehouse
notes: Indiana's only three-sheet facility. Opened 2010. Formerly SportONE Parkview Icehouse. Home of Fort Wayne Komets (ECHL) practice, Indiana Tech Warriors (NCAA), Fort Wayne Spacemen (USPHL), Fort Wayne Youth Hockey, and High School Hockey Association. Overtime Bar + Grill overlooks all three rinks. 12 locker rooms. On-site pro shop.

name: Perry Park Ice Rink
notes: Established 1961. Indy Parks' only indoor ice rink. NHL-size sheet, 4 locker rooms, concession stand. Seasonal: September through April. Home to South Stars HS Hockey Club, South Indy Youth Hockey Association, Total Package Hockey Center of Excellence, Sycamore Ice Skating Club. Admission: children $5, adults $7, skate rental $3.

name: Swonder Ice Arena
notes: Opened Oct 2002. Two NHL-size sheets, 1,500 seating. 10 locker rooms. 10,000 sq ft fitness center with running track upstairs. Home to Evansville Youth Hockey, Tri-State Adult Hockey, River City Ice Theatre, Greater Evansville Figure Skating Club, USI Ice Eagles. One sheet open year-round. City of Evansville operated.

name: Terre Haute Outdoor Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink (location varies by year — The Meadows or downtown). Terre Haute Parks operated. No dedicated phone.

name: The Kube Sports Complex
notes: Two full-size indoor sheets (Ice Kube). Bar and lounge with overhead ice views. Trampoline park on-site. Little Blackhawks Learn to Play partner. 25 min south of Chicago off I-80/94.

name: Thunder Ice Arena
notes: Opened 2017. 46,200 sq ft, 700-seat arena. Home of Trine Thunder NCAA DIII men's and women's hockey (NCHA). Only women's intercollegiate hockey program in Indiana. Steuben County YMCA and local schools also use facility. Pro shop, VIP lounge, weight/training rooms. Public skating and youth leagues.

name: Valpo Ice Rink
notes: Outdoor refrigerated rink under 12,000 sq ft open-air William E. Urschel Pavilion at Central Park Plaza. 80'x120' real ice. Mid-November through early March. Warming area, concessions, sound system, colored lighting. Open hockey nights. Skate rental available.

name: KC Ice Center
registration_label: Kansas City Stars / KC Jets Youth Hockey
notes: Two surfaces: indoor NHL-size rink (year-round, 800 spectator seating) + outdoor covered NHL-size Ice Pavilion (seasonal Nov–early Mar, added 2012, $1.5M). Formerly Ice Sports KC. $1M indoor renovation 2011. Home of KC Stars and KC Jets Youth Hockey Association. Summer: Pavilion used for inline hockey (KC Cobras). ⚠️ CSV listed as 1 sheet Indoor — corrected to 2 surfaces (1 indoor + 1 covered outdoor seasonal).

name: Wichita Ice Center
notes: Opened 1996 (formerly Ice Sports Wichita). Two sheets: Olympic-size + NHL-size. 2,000 capacity. Full-length viewing windows. Pucks Sports Bar, pro sport store, party rooms, concessions. Youth and adult hockey leagues, figure skating academy, learn-to-skate, speed skating. Operated by Sports Facilities Companies. Public skate $7.

name: Alpine Ice Arena
notes: Operating since 1960. Seasonal indoor rink (typically fall through spring). Hockey leagues, tournaments, clinics, and public skating. One of Louisville's historic ice facilities.

name: Broadbent Arena
notes: Multi-purpose expo arena. Ice installed for events only — not a dedicated hockey facility. Part of Kentucky Exposition Center complex.

name: Edge Ice Center
notes: Year-round community rink operated by Owensboro Parks. Public skating and hockey leagues.

name: Iceland Sports Complex
notes: Two-sheet year-round facility. Founded 2000. LiveBarn equipped. Full hockey programming: public skate, youth and adult leagues, pickup hockey, broomball. Home of University of Louisville Cards Ice Hockey, Louisville Skating Academy (figure skating), and Louisville Stallions youth hockey. Pro shop on-site.

name: Lexington Ice Center
notes: Two-sheet year-round facility. Full hockey programming and public skating. Serves Lexington and central Kentucky.

name: Northern Kentucky Ice Center
notes: Two ice surfaces: full-size NHL rink + smaller studio rink. Founded 1992. Only year-round indoor rink in Northern Kentucky. Home of Northern Kentucky Skating Club (NKSC) and Northern Kentucky Youth Hockey Association (NKYHA). Public skating, hockey leagues, figure skating, learn-to-skate. Admission $10, skate rental $3.

name: SoKY Ice Rink
notes: Seasonal outdoor rink at SoKY Marketplace. Hockey availability unconfirmed — primarily recreational public skating. ⚠️ Phone unverified. ⚠️ PLACEHOLDER URL — links to tourism site.

name: Hirsch Memorial Coliseum
notes: Seasonal ice for NAHL Shreveport Mudbugs. Not a permanent year-round rink. Also hosts concerts and events. ⚠️ Website listed is Georges Pond (NAHL team site) — verify dedicated arena website.

name: Planet Ice
notes: Only permanent indoor ice rink in Louisiana. Opened 2011. Home of Louisiana Drillers (NA3HL), LAHA Youth Hockey (ages 4–16), Figure Skating Club of Louisiana (USFSA member since 2019), Baton Rouge United Hockey Association (BRUHA), and the Bayou Battle League. Fraser Cup champion facility (2025). Public skating, learn-to-skate, birthday parties. ⚠️ CSV listed website as planeticearena.com — corrected to planeticerinkla.com.

name: Raising Cane's River Center Arena
notes: Event-based ice — no permanent sheet. Home of Baton Rouge Zydeco (FPHL Continental Hockey League). Ice installed for hockey season and special events only.

name: Amesbury Sports Park Ice Rink
notes: Community rink.

name: Archbishop Williams HS Rink
notes: High school rink. Clinics and rentals.

name: Armstrong Arena
notes: DCR rink in Plymouth.

name: Asiaf Arena
notes: DCR rink in Brockton.

name: Assumption University Ice Rink
notes: College rink. Camps and clinics.

name: Babson Skating Center
notes: Babson College rink with public access, camps, and clinics. Full hockey and figure skating programming.

name: Blackstone Valley IcePlex
notes: Two NHL sheets. Full programming.

name: Boch Ice Center
notes: Two NHL sheets. Youth and adult leagues. LiveBarn.

name: Breakaway Ice Center
notes: Two NHL sheets. Major youth hockey facility. LiveBarn.

name: Bridgewater Ice Arena
notes: Two NHL sheets. Full programming.

name: Brooks School Rink
notes: Prep school rink. Camps and clinics.

name: Buffone Skating Arena
notes: DCR rink in Worcester.

name: Canton Ice House
notes: Two NHL sheets. Youth and adult leagues. LiveBarn.

name: Chelmsford Forum
notes: Valley Rinks network (per mass.gov, this DCR rink is operated by Valley Rinks). LiveBarn.

name: Collins-Moylan Skating Arena
notes: DCR rink in Greenfield.

name: Connell Rink

Home to St. Norbert College Green Knights (NCAA DIII), Green Bay Gamblers (USHL) practices, GBAYHA, and DC Everest youth programs. 4th sheet (Donald & Patricia Schneider Family Arena, 3,000 seats) under construction — expected fall 2026. Has hosted 8 USA Hockey National Championships.

name: Crawford Center

name: Dodgeville Ice Arena
notes: Small-town rink serving Dodgeville and surrounding rural communities.

name: Don Fanetti Ice Arena

name: Eagle River Sports Arena
notes: Wisconsin's oldest indoor hockey arena, built 1933. Known as "The Dome" — iconic wooden lamella truss roof on the National Register of Historic Places. Two sheets: original dome (NHL, ~2,000 seats) + Olympic sheet added 1995-96. Home to Northland Pines Eagles, Eagle River Recreation Association youth hockey, Eagle River Figure Skating Club, and the Wisconsin Hockey Hall of Fame (est. 1975). Pro shop on site.

name: Eble Ice Arena
notes: Popular suburban rink with strong youth hockey presence. Managed by Waukesha County Parks.

name: Edwards Ice Arena
notes: City-owned seasonal rink open October through March. Home to Beloit Youth Hockey Association (Jr. Knights), Beloit Memorial High School hockey, Rock County Fury girls hockey, and Greater Beloit Special Olympics. Public skating Fri/Sat evenings and Sunday afternoons.

name: Elroy Lions Club Ice Rink
notes: Volunteer-maintained outdoor rink. Weather-dependent.

name: Fond du Lac Blue Line Family Ice Center

name: Fort Atkinson Ice Arena
notes: Community rink serving Fort Atkinson and Jefferson County.

name: Green Island Ice Arena

name: Greenheck Turner Community Center

Home to DC Everest High School Hockey, Everest Youth Hockey (EYH), and Timberline Figure Skating Club. Full multi-sport facility with fitness center, gyms, turf fields, and batting cages. Also known as Greenheck Field House.

name: Hartmeyer Ice Arena

Needs ~$3M in repairs by 2028. Home to Patriots Youth Hockey, Madison Eastside Lakers (La Follette/East HS co-op), Monona Grove HS hockey, Figure Skating Club of Madison, and adaptive skating program. Year-round operations. Also home to Wisconsin Whalers (NA3HL).

name: Hayward Sports Center
notes: Known for hosting major youth tournaments in the Northwoods. Strong community support.

name: Hobbs Municipal Ice Center
spectator_notes: Main rink seats 1,400; second rink seats 500.

Home to UW-Eau Claire Blugolds men's and women's hockey (NCAA DIII) and Eau Claire Youth Hockey. LiveBarn available (youth games; UWEC streams privately). Fitness center, mezzanine viewing, meeting/party rooms.

name: Howard G. Mullett Ice Center
notes: Donated to Arrowhead School District in 1998 by Donald Mullett in honor of his father. Non-profit community arena. Located on Arrowhead High School campus. Public skating, learn to skate, youth and adult hockey. Reverse osmosis water treatment for superior ice quality.

name: Hudson Sports & Civic Center
notes: Two sheets: Gornick Rink and Drewiske Rink (both NHL-size). Home of Hudson Hockey Association and Hudson High School JV/Varsity hockey teams. Also hosts figure skating, tournaments, trade shows, and community events. Community room with commercial kitchen available for rentals. Hudson has won 6 Wisconsin state hockey championships.

name: K.B. Willett Ice Arena

name: Kenosha Ice Arena

name: Madison Ice Arena

name: Mandt Community Center

Also hosts games at MSOE Kern Center.

name: Ponds of Brookfield

Hosts Autumn Ice Classic and CCM Elite Youth tournaments. Also sled hockey (adaptive). Facility includes full waterpark, competition pool, fitness center, and fieldhouse.

name: Reedsburg Winter Park Ice Rink

Recent renovations include new boards/glass, icemaking equipment, flooring, and sound system.

name: Wilson Ice Arena

name: Woodman's Sports & Convention Center

Two ice sheets: 1,500-seat permanent main rink (Mercyhealth Arena) + multipurpose arena (250-seat, seasonal ice). Home to Janesville Jets (NAHL), Beloit College hockey, and Janesville Figure Skating Club. Replaces the old Janesville Ice Arena (1974–2025) which is now closed.

name: Hope Gas Ice Pavilion at Mylan Park

notes: Opened Sept 8, 2025. 48,000 sq ft. NHL-sized surface (some sources say Olympic-sized). 906–1,200 permanent seats. 8 locker rooms, 2 meeting rooms, concessions, skate rental area. $15-16M project funded by Hope Gas, Hazel Ruby McQuain Charitable Trust, Mylan Park Foundation. Home of WVU Club Hockey (5 teams), Morgantown Hockey Association, area high school teams. Public skating Sat/Sun. Curling and learn-to-skate programming. Designed to accommodate a 2nd sheet if demand warrants. ⚠️ CSV listed as 'Hope Gas Ice Arena' — correct name is Hope Gas Ice Pavilion.

name: Morgantown Ice Arena

notes: City of Morgantown / BOPARC facility at White Park. 400-seat, 185'×85' sheet. Open Oct–Feb following Hope Gas Ice Pavilion opening (WVU Hockey and MHA moved to Mylan Park Sept 2025). Seasonal community public skating. Public skate $5/$6; skate rental $3. Wed daytime special $6 includes skates. ⚠️ CSV phone (304) 292-6865 — corrected to (304) 296-8356 per BOPARC website.
